creator: Alexander Terp
description:
The `in` operator inconsistently coerces types when checking string containment. int is silently coerced to string, but float and bool are not.

**Repro:**
```
print(1 in "hello123")     // true (int coerced to "1")
print(3.14 in "3.14159")   // Error (float not coerced)
print(true in "truefalse")  // Error (bool not coerced)
```

Either all should coerce or none should.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/49_int_in_string.rad`, `51_in_coercion.rad`, `51b_in_coercion2.rad`
title: int in str coerces but float in str and bool in str do not
